rationale: This page provides a concise summary of the UNESCO Memory of the World (MoW) Programme, including its history, objectives, organizational structure, and India's specific involvement through IGNCA. This factual content is suitable for generating study questions.
cleanedText:
About UNESCO Memory of the World (MoW) Programme
* Genesis: Launched in 1992.
* Objectives: Facilitate preservation (particularly in areas affected by conflict and/or natural disaster), catalyse universal access, and enhance public awareness worldwide of the significance of documentary heritage.
* International Advisory Committee (IAC) is the main body responsible for advising UNESCO on the planning and implementation of programme as a whole.
* IAC comprises 14 members serving in a personal capacity, appointed by the Director-General of UNESCO.
* India's Nodal Agency (for coordination): Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA) since 2014.
* Current Status: 570 Memory of the World inscriptions on the International Register since 1997.
